batch_summary.note: Aug 2025: 1 RF-11 primary flag. S001 — counselor relay + child-as-parental-support-judge. Arc initialized at 1 signal (CANDIDATE).

primary_flags.cross_rf: RF-04 (COMP-RF04-024, B-027 Aug 14-15 mega-cluster); RF-08 (counselor-relay mechanism)
primary_flags.message_text: ROW-14097: 'She\'s told the counselor before how she\'s afraid to talk to you about things and how she feels cause you would just yell at her and not listen' | ROW-14146: 'so she doesn\'t feel like you started another family and pushed her out' | ROW-14207: 'She sees who supports her'
primary_flags.summary: Aug 2025: Melissa deploys Olivia's therapeutic statements as adult-conflict ammunition against Eli within the Aug 14-15 mega-cluster. Three-component RF-11 mechanism: (1) ROW-14097 counselor relay — Melissa cites Olivia's statements to her therapist as evidence of Eli's inadequacy ('afraid to talk to you'), positioning the therapeutic relationship as a corroborating source for the inadequate-father narrative. Per ADD-004 behavioral context: therapy non-continuity pattern (Melissa drops therapists when called out) suggests strategic therapeutic engagement rather than genuine treatment. (2) ROW-14146 — Olivia's alleged emotional displacement ('started another family and pushed her out') deployed as guilt framing: Olivia's therapeutic expression weaponized in adult conflict. (3) ROW-14207 'She sees who supports her' — child positioned as referee of parental support quality; Olivia placed in the role of judging which parent is the better parent. ADD-006 context: Olivia-as-stabilizer pattern confirmed at CP2 — counselor relay is documentary evidence of the broader parentification pattern. Compound COMP-RF11-001: RF-11+RF-04+RF-08 — counselor relay is both RF-08 mechanism (relay-through-therapeutic-content) and RF-04 inadequacy-weapon; RF-11 primary because child's therapeutic context is directly weaponized in adult conflict.

arc_updates.event: S001 — arc initialized at first signal
